Title: Record single observation with valid information
Objective: Verify single observation entry for a student across all 4 subjects with valid inputs.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
Currently on the Scan Management screen.
Student "Nguyen Van A" exists in the system.
TestData:
Student Name: "Nguyen Van A"
Observation Date: Current date
Subjects: IT, PLT, English, Digital Arts
Ratings: G, S, NI, Empty
Notes: IT: Good logic; PLT: Active participation; English: Good communication; DA: Creative UI
Steps:
1. Select student "Nguyen Van A" from the student list.
2. Select current date as the observation date.
3. For IT: Enter note "Good logic", system suggests "G", accept the rating.
4. For PLT: Enter note "Active participation", system suggests "S", accept the rating.
5. For English: Enter note "Good communication", system suggests "NI", accept the rating.
6. For Digital Arts: Enter note "Creative UI", leave rating unselected (empty).
7. Click the "Save Observation" button.
ExpectedResult:
Success notification is displayed.
The new observation is successfully saved to Google Sheets with ratings and notes for all 4 subjects.
The student's completion status is updated on the UI.

Title: Auto rating suggestion based on notes
Objective: Verify that the system automatically suggests an appropriate rating approximately 400 milliseconds after notes are entered.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
Currently on the observation entry screen for a student.
Steps:
1. Select IT subject.
2. Enter observation note: "Excellent performance" into the notes field.
3. Stop typing and wait for approximately 400 milliseconds.
ExpectedResult: The system automatically analyzes the note content and suggests rating "G".

Title: Allow user to overwrite auto-suggested rating
Objective: Verify that the user can manually change the rating after the auto-suggestion has been made.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
Currently on the observation entry screen for a student.
TestData:
Notes: Excellent performance
Overwrite rating: S
Steps:
1. Select IT subject.
2. Enter observation note "Excellent performance" and wait for the system to suggest rating "G".
3. Manually select rating "S" from the rating list.
ExpectedResult: The rating is successfully updated to "S" and is not auto-reverted back to "G".

Title: Batch observation entry for all subjects
Objective: Verify bulk observation entry using the spreadsheet-style layout for multiple students simultaneously.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
Currently on the Batch Entry - All Subjects screen.
An active batch "PNV26A" exists with 20 students.
TestData:
Batch Name: "PNV26A"
Enter data for 18 students; leave 2 students completely blank.
Steps:
1. Select batch "PNV26A".
2. Select observation date.
3. Enter ratings and notes for 18 students in the spreadsheet-style grid.
4. Leave all fields empty for the remaining 2 students.
5. Click "Save Batch".
ExpectedResult:
Success notification is displayed: "Saved 18 students; Skipped 2 students".
Observation details for the 18 students are saved to Google Sheets.
The 2 skipped students have no data written.

Title: Batch observation entry by specific subject
Objective: Verify batch observation entry for a single subject for multiple students simultaneously.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
Currently on the Batch Entry - By Subject screen.
TestData:
Batch Name: "PNV26A"
Subject: English
Enter ratings and notes for the English column for multiple students.
Steps:
1. Select batch "PNV26A".
2. Select subject "English".
3. Enter ratings and notes under the English column for the students.
4. Click "Save".
ExpectedResult:
Only English observation data for the entered students is updated in Google Sheets.
Other subjects' observation data for the students remains untouched.

Title: Edit ratings and notes of an existing observation
Objective: Verify updating an existing observation's ratings and notes while keeping the observation date locked.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
An observation record already exists for student "Nguyen Van A" on the current date.
Currently on the edit observation screen.
TestData:
New IT rating: NI
New IT notes: Needs more focus
Steps:
1. Select student "Nguyen Van A" and the current observation date.
2. Verify if the Observation Date field is locked (read-only).
3. Change IT rating from "G" to "NI".
4. Update IT note to "Needs more focus".
5. Click "Save" or "Update".
ExpectedResult:
The Observation Date field is locked (read-only) and cannot be edited.
The record is successfully updated in Google Sheets with the new rating and note.

Title: Duplicate detection - Merge when <3 subjects are completed
Objective: Verify that the system automatically merges a new observation into an existing record when the existing record has less than 3 subjects completed.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
An observation record already exists for student "Nguyen Van A" on the current date, with only 2 subjects completed (e.g. IT and PLT).
TestData:
Student Name: "Nguyen Van A"
Date: Current Date
New Subject: English
Rating: G
Notes: Good reading skills
Steps:
1. Click to add a new observation for student "Nguyen Van A" on the current date.
2. Enter English subject details: rating "G" and note "Good reading skills".
3. Click "Save".
ExpectedResult:
System automatically detects duplication and performs a merge.
Success message is shown: "Added to existing session."
The merged record in Google Sheets now has completed data for all 3 subjects (IT, PLT, English).

Title: Duplicate detection - Reject when >=3 subjects are completed
Objective: Verify that the system rejects a new observation record and displays an error when trying to create a new record for a student with 3 or more subjects already completed on the same day.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
An observation record already exists for student "Nguyen Van A" on the current date, with 3 subjects already completed (IT, PLT, English).
TestData:
Student Name: "Nguyen Van A"
Date: Current Date
New Subject: Digital Arts
Rating: S
Notes: Nice color scheme
Steps:
1. Click to add a new observation for student "Nguyen Van A" on the current date.
2. Enter Digital Arts subject details: rating "S" and note "Nice color scheme".
3. Click "Save".
ExpectedResult:
System rejects the entry and displays error message: "A feedback record for this date already exists."
The duplicate data is not saved to Google Sheets, and the user remains on the entry screen.

Title: Complete observation session and auto-fill N/A for missing observations
Objective: Verify marking a session as complete, checking auto-fill of N/A and locking of the session.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
An active observation session for the current date is in "Incomplete" status.
Student "Nguyen Van B" has no observation notes recorded in this session.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the session management screen.
2. Click "Mark Session Complete" for the current date's session.
ExpectedResult:
The session status changes to "Complete".
Student "Nguyen Van B" automatically receives "N/A" for their ratings/notes.
The system locks the session, preventing any further observation creation or edits.

Title: Display session progress indicators for a batch
Objective: Verify that the system accurately calculates and displays observation completion statistics and progress.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
Batch "PNV26A" has 20 students, and observations have been saved for 10 students.
Steps: 1. Open the Session Progress screen for batch "PNV26A" on the current date.
ExpectedResult:
The system displays a completion progress of 50% (10/20 students observed).
Progress indicators and statistical counts are correct and dynamically updated.

Title: Concurrent editing resolution from two teachers (Three-Way Merge)
Objective: Verify that non-conflicting concurrent edits are merged and a warning is generated for conflicting fields.
Preconditions:
Two staff members (Staff A and Staff B) are logged in using different accounts.
Both open the observation record of student "Nguyen Van A" for the current date.
The initial record has IT notes set to "Good performance".
TestData:
Staff B changes IT note to "Excellent performance"
Staff A changes IT note to "Very good performance" and adds English note "English Comment added"
Steps:
1. Staff B edits the IT note to "Excellent performance" and clicks Save first.
2. Staff A edits the IT note to "Very good performance" and adds English note "English Comment added", then clicks Save.
ExpectedResult:
System applies Three-Way Merge: Staff A's new English note is successfully saved.
However, the IT note retains Staff B's value ("Excellent performance").
Staff A receives a warning: "Saved, but some fields were updated by another teacher."

Title: Search, filter, and sort student observation list
Objective: Verify searching by name, filtering by completion status, and sorting the list of student records.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
The student list contains diverse names (e.g. Nguyen Van A, Tran Thi B, Le Van C...) with mixed observation statuses (Completed, Incomplete).
TestData:
Search keyword: "Nguyen"
Status filter: Incomplete
Sorting: Alphabetical (A-Z)
Steps:
1. Enter "Nguyen" in the search box.
2. Set the status filter to "Incomplete".
3. Set the sorting selection to alphabetical A-Z.
ExpectedResult:
The list displays only students whose name contains "Nguyen" and who are in "Incomplete" observation status.
The list is correctly sorted from A to Z.

Title: Dashboard metrics and automatic refresh
Objective: Verify that the dashboard displays complete stats and updates in real-time when observation data changes.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
Currently on the Dashboard Overview screen.
Observe the initial completed sessions count.
Steps:
1. Check all displayed metrics on the dashboard (Total Batches, Total Students, Active Sessions, Completed Sessions, etc.).
2. Go to the Session Management screen and complete 1 active session.
3. Return to the Dashboard Overview screen and verify the metrics.
ExpectedResult:
1. All metrics display accurate statistics.
2. Completed Sessions count increases by 1, and the Observation Completion Rate is updated accordingly.

Title: Required fields validation for single observation
Objective: Verify system validation error when trying to save with missing required fields (Date, Student).
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
Currently on the single observation entry screen.
TestData:
Student: Empty
Observation Date: Empty
IT rating: G
Steps:
1. Leave Student and Observation Date fields empty.
2. Select IT rating "G".
3. Click "Save Observation".
ExpectedResult:
System displays a validation error message prompting to enter Student and Observation Date.
The record is not saved to the system.

Title: Invalid rating value validation
Objective: Verify that the system prevents saving rating values other than G, S, NI, or empty.
Preconditions:
User is logged in with Staff role.
Currently on the single observation entry screen for student "Nguyen Van A".
Steps:
1. Enter observation note for IT.
2. Forcefully enter/send rating value "A" (which is outside the allowed list: G, S, NI, empty).
3. Click "Save".
ExpectedResult: System displays a validation error (e.g. "Rating must be G, S, NI or Empty") and rejects the save operation.
